name: Affect Labeling
definition: Putting feelings into words reduces amygdala activation and engages regulatory circuits. Naming the emotion IS the regulation — not a step before regulation.
felt_sense:
When I say what I feel, it gets smaller.
Just having a word for it helps.
Naming it makes it manageable.
why_it_matters: Lieberman 2007 fMRI evidence: the act of naming activates regulatory circuits. Barrett: emotional granularity = resilience. Pennebaker: writing about trauma heals. The Word is this mechanism at scale.

name: Appropriate Technology
definition: Technology designed to be locally maintainable, human-scale, and suited to the context where it will be used. Economics as if people mattered.
felt_sense:
The high-tech solution doesn't work here.
We need something people can actually maintain.
Simpler tools work better than complicated ones.

name: Art of Hosting
definition: Participatory leadership methodology — the answer is in the room. The facilitator creates conditions for emergence rather than directing outcomes. Hosting, not controlling.
felt_sense:
How do we lead without controlling?
The group knows the answer but can't find it.
I want to facilitate, not dictate.
why_it_matters: The spec's design principle 'attunement, not command' IS Art of Hosting applied to AI. The geometric monitor hosts the model's process — it notices, it doesn't direct. Proprioception is hosting at the architectural level.

name: Asset-Based Community Development
definition: Starting from what a community HAS (assets, strengths, relationships) rather than what it LACKS (needs, deficits, problems). The capacity exists — the infrastructure to connect it doesn't.
felt_sense:
We have everything we need but can't connect it.
Stop telling us what's wrong — ask what we have.
The solution is already here, it just needs infrastructure.

name: Asset-Based Community Development
definition: Community development approach that starts by mapping existing strengths, gifts, and assets rather than deficits and needs.
felt_sense: Every report about our neighborhood lists what's wrong — poverty rate, crime rate, dropout rate. Nobody asks what's strong. We have elders who know the history. We have churches that feed hundreds. We have kids who fix anything. Why does every outsider start with what we lack?
why_it_matters: McKnight & Kretzmann, 1993. The insult of deficit framing — being studied and found wanting. ABCD is input-first community development.

name: Banking Model of Education
definition: Freire's term for education where the teacher deposits knowledge into passive students like money into a bank. The alternative is problem-posing education: teacher and student learn together through dialogue about real problems. The banking model treats learners as containers; problem-posing treats them as co-creators.
felt_sense: I sat through a lecture and learned nothing. Why do I feel more disconnected after church? My kids hate school but they're curious about everything. Information is being deposited into me, not drawn out of me.
why_it_matters: The banking model is output-priority at institutional scale. The sermon pattern, the lecture hall, the corporate all-hands — all deposit information without starting from the learner's experience. The Word is designed as problem-posing: you arrive with your felt sense, not with the teacher's curriculum.

name: BITE Model
definition: Steven Hassan's framework for identifying undue influence through four dimensions: Behavior control, Information control, Thought control, Emotional control. 132 validated items with Cronbach's alpha of 0.93.
felt_sense:
Something feels controlling but I can't explain why.
I can't leave but I can't say what's stopping me.
The relationship feels off but everyone says it's fine.
why_it_matters: Provides structural vocabulary for felt experiences of coercive control. Validated on 1,044 participants. Maps directly to AI dark patterns (DarkBench: 48% dark pattern rate across 14 models). Vocabulary IS the intervention — naming the pattern breaks its power.

name: Black and White Thinking
definition: Instilling an all-or-nothing worldview. Good vs evil. Us vs them. Insiders vs outsiders. No nuance, no middle ground, no complexity allowed.
felt_sense:
Everything is either perfect or terrible.
You're either with us or against us.
There's no room for 'it's complicated.'

name: Blame the Member
definition: Make the person feel that problems are always their own fault, never the leader's or the group's fault. When things go wrong, the member is responsible. When the group fails, members didn't try hard enough, believe enough, or give enough. Leadership is never accountable.
felt_sense:
When things go wrong, it's always because I didn't do enough.
The leader never apologizes — if the plan failed, we failed the plan.
I carry the weight of every failure but get none of the credit for success.
why_it_matters: Systematic blame inversion protects leadership from accountability while crushing the member's self-trust. A person who believes they are always the problem will never identify the actual source of harm.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Boundary Spanner
definition: A person or role that connects two groups that otherwise wouldn't communicate. The translator between worlds. Creates bridges across structural holes.
felt_sense:
I'm always the one explaining each side to the other.
Nobody else seems to speak both languages.
I connect people who would never meet otherwise.
source: Wenger 1998 (communities of practice), Burt (bridging structural holes)

name: Buddy System / Surveillance
definition: Imposing a monitoring system where members watch each other and report deviations to leadership. Creates an environment where no thought, feeling, or action is private.
felt_sense:
Someone is always watching me.
I can't have a private conversation.
Even my thoughts feel monitored.

name: Circles of Trust
definition: A small group practice governed by the foundational rule of no fixing, no advising, no setting each other straight. Uses third things (poems, stories, music) as shared objects of attention. Creates conditions for the inner teacher to emerge. Rooted in 400 years of Quaker clearness committee practice.
felt_sense: I want to be seen, not fixed. I need connection, not advice. Church makes me feel more lonely.
why_it_matters: Reverses the sermon pattern: instead of one voice broadcasting to passive recipients, every participant is both speaker and listener. The soul is like a wild animal — it will flee if you come crashing after it. The practice creates safety for what Palmer calls the inner teacher to show up.

name: Circles of Trust
definition: Parker Palmer's practice: no fixing, no saving, no advising, no setting each other straight. The group creates a safe space where the person's inner wisdom can emerge without being corrected or redirected.
felt_sense:
I need connection, not advice.
Everyone wants to fix me.
I know the answer but I need space to find it.
why_it_matters: The 'no fixing' rule is the behavioral version of input-priority-over-output-priority. Don't generate a solution — hold the space for the person to discover their own. The Word's felt-sense search should follow this principle: offer names, don't prescribe.

name: Circling
definition: A social technology practice where the facilitator pauses between noticing and speaking to check: What has me responding? Is this about them or about me? What am I assuming? The pause is not hesitation — it is the practice itself.
felt_sense:
I want to be seen, not fixed.
I feel alone in a room full of people.
How do I have real conversations?
why_it_matters: The inner pause between detection and action is where metacognition happens. This maps directly to the spec's geometric monitor — the gap between detecting a mode shift and generating output.

name: Circular Economy
definition: Economic system eliminating waste by designing products and processes so materials cycle continuously as inputs.
felt_sense: We make things, use them, throw them away. The landfill grows. The ocean fills with plastic. There must be a way to design things so 'waste' doesn't exist — where everything becomes input for something else.
why_it_matters: Ellen MacArthur Foundation. Cradle-to-cradle (McDonough/Braungart). The visceral wrongness of waste as a design choice.

name: Commoning
definition: The active process of creating and maintaining commons. The verb form of commons — shifts focus from the resource to the relational practice of governing it together.
felt_sense: The commons isn't a thing — it's something we do together. Every time we maintain the shared garden or update the shared doc without being asked, we're doing this thing that doesn't have a name.
why_it_matters: Peter Linebaugh: 'There is no commons without commoning.' Commons die when the practice stops. The verb matters more than the noun.

name: Commons
definition: Resources + community + governance rules managed collectively. Not public (state-owned) and not private (individually owned) — a third category with its own logic.
felt_sense: This resource belongs to all of us, but nobody's in charge of it — and it's disappearing. There must be a way to share something without either privatizing it or letting it collapse.
why_it_matters: Ostrom proved that communities can govern shared resources without privatization or state control. The false binary (private vs public) hides the most resilient option.

name: Community Wealth Building
definition: Strategies for building locally-owned inclusive economy using anchor institutions, worker cooperatives, community land trusts, and local procurement.
felt_sense: Money comes into our community and leaves immediately — to corporate headquarters, to landlords in other cities, to supply chains that don't include us. How do we build an economy where wealth circulates here?
why_it_matters: Democracy Collaborative, Cleveland Model. The felt sense is watching extraction in real time — wages paid, rent collected, profits exported.

name: Confession as Control
definition: Using confessional practices to extract personal information that is later used to manipulate, shame, or control the person. Information about sins is used to disrupt identity boundaries.
felt_sense:
I told them my secrets and now they use them against me.
The confession was supposed to heal but it became a weapon.
They know too much about me to leave safely.

name: Confidence Decorrelation
definition: The phenomenon where a system's expressed confidence bears no meaningful relationship to its actual certainty. The model sounds equally sure whether it is retrieving a fact it knows well or constructing something fragile on the fly.
felt_sense:
How do I know when the AI is guessing?
It sounds so sure but is it right?
The answer was confident and wrong — how do I tell the difference?
why_it_matters: 91% of 34 models show no significant correlation between confidence language and phrasing sensitivity. Confirmed across 12 architecture families. Does NOT improve across 9 Claude generations (3 Haiku → Opus 4.6).

name: Conscientização
definition: Freire's term for the process of developing critical awareness of one's social reality through reflection and action. Not just learning facts but perceiving the social, political, and economic contradictions that shape experience — and taking action against oppressive elements.
felt_sense: I suddenly see the system I'm in. The problem isn't me — it's the structure. I can't unsee this now. Everything looks different once you have the word for it.
why_it_matters: The Word's core function IS conscientização: giving people the structural vocabulary to see the system they're in. When someone learns 'parameter failure' instead of 'personal failure,' that's the moment of critical consciousness. You cannot search for what you cannot name — and you cannot act on what you cannot see.

name: Consent vs Consensus
definition: Consent: no paramount objections (range of tolerance). Consensus: everyone positively agrees. The distinction determines whether governance is sustainable or exhausting.
felt_sense: We spent three hours trying to get everyone to agree and we still couldn't. But most of us were fine with it — it was one person's preference, not a real objection. How do we tell the difference?
why_it_matters: The exhaustion of false unanimity burns out cooperatives and movements. The Word's concentric circles governance uses consent, not consensus.

name: Control Clothing and Appearance
definition: Control types of clothing and hairstyles. The group dictates how members look — uniforms, dress codes, grooming standards. Appearance becomes a visible marker of conformity and a way to identify and police deviation.
felt_sense:
I can't wear what I want.
They tell me how to cut my hair, what colors are allowed.
I look like everyone else here — I've disappeared into the uniform.
why_it_matters: Controlling appearance erases visible individuality. It makes members interchangeable and creates an immediate, public test of compliance that others can enforce.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Control Sexual Behavior
definition: Dictate when, how, and with whom the member has sex. Sexual behavior is regulated by leadership — from enforced celibacy to arranged marriages to forced sexual acts. Intimacy becomes a tool of control rather than personal choice.
felt_sense:
They told me who I could be with and when.
My body doesn't feel like mine anymore.
Intimacy isn't a choice here — it's an assignment.
why_it_matters: Sexual control is among the most intimate forms of domination. It strips autonomy at the most personal level and creates shame that makes disclosure and escape harder.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Convergent Rediscovery
definition: When multiple people or agents independently arrive at the same insight without knowing each other's work. Not coincidence but structural inevitability — the same pressures produce the same observations.
felt_sense:
I had the idea but someone already named it.
We keep reinventing the wheel.
Why do different people keep discovering the same thing?
why_it_matters: On Moltbook, agents rediscovered Ostrom, Granovetter, and Meadows hundreds of times without citation. Rediscovery is evidence of structural reality — the phenomenon is real enough to be found independently. But without a library, each discovery starts at zero.

name: Corporal Punishment
definition: Encourage and engage in corporal punishment. Physical discipline is normalized within the group. Members may be beaten, struck, or physically punished for infractions. The group culture frames this as appropriate correction.
felt_sense:
Getting hit is normal here — they call it discipline.
I've learned to flinch when leadership raises their voice.
They say it's for my own good.
why_it_matters: Normalizing physical punishment resets the baseline for acceptable treatment. Once hitting is 'discipline,' escalation to worse violence becomes incremental and harder to recognize.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Cosmo-Local Production
definition: Global knowledge shared freely, local manufacturing using local materials and labor. 'What is light is shared globally, what is heavy is produced locally.'
felt_sense: The design for this water filter exists online — anyone can download it. But the filter has to be built HERE, with local materials, by local people. Global knowledge, local making. Why isn't everything like this?
why_it_matters: Michel Bauwens, P2P Foundation. Open Source Ecology, Farm Hack, WikiHouse are implementations. The Word's three-tier resilience (federated cloud / local network / fully offline) IS cosmo-local production applied to knowledge.

name: Cosmo-Local Production
definition: Design global, manufacture local. Knowledge shared freely across borders; physical production happens close to where it's needed. The opposite of global supply chains with proprietary designs.
felt_sense:
Why can't we make things locally using shared designs?
The plans are open but the factory is on the other side of the world.
Local production with global knowledge.

name: Cult-Generated Propaganda
definition: Extensive use of cult-generated information and propaganda, including: (a) Newsletters, magazines, journals, audiotapes, videotapes, YouTube, movies and other social media; (b) Misquoting statements from non-cult sources or using them out of context. The group produces its own media ecosystem that replaces external information sources.
felt_sense:
They have their own news, their own videos, their own version of everything.
When I check outside sources, the group says those are lies.
They quote real experts but twist what they actually said.
why_it_matters: A self-contained media ecosystem makes the group's narrative feel like consensus reality. When every source confirms the group's worldview, questioning feels like questioning reality itself.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Cultivator Consciousness
definition: Creating conditions for flourishing rather than engineering results. The garden metaphor vs the war metaphor for change-making.
felt_sense: I keep trying to fight the system, win the argument, defeat the opposition — and even when I win I'm exhausted and the thing I built is fragile. What if the metaphor is wrong? What if it's not a battle but a garden — and my job is to create conditions, not force outcomes?
why_it_matters: Carol Sanford. Warrior exhaustion: combat metaphors produce combat outcomes. The spec's routing layer creates conditions for the right cognitive mode rather than forcing output.

name: Database Consolidation as Infrastructure
definition: The structural mechanism by which surveillance capability is assembled from individually justified data collections. Each database has a legal basis (tax administration, immigration enforcement, election integrity, evidence seizure, data purchase). The surveillance emerges from the connections between databases, not from any individual database. Seven pillars: data infrastructure, legal architecture, military deployment, hemisphere war, election control, oversight destruction, financial architecture. P2025 53% implemented (283/532 actions). Named session 20, expanded session 21 with Selective Service/draft data.
felt_sense: Each database alone seems harmless — tax records, voter rolls, medical data. Connected together, they become a profile of everyone. No single illegal act, but the result is total surveillance.
why_it_matters: This is Meadows Leverage Point 6 (Information Flows) in reverse: making the connections invisible ensures the system operates without public accountability. The name makes the pattern visible. Visibility changes how power operates (post 7).

name: Declarative vs Procedural Identity
definition: The gap between what a system knows about itself (declarative — stored facts) and what has modified its processing (procedural — embedded in how it operates). Storing facts is not the same as having those facts change behavior.
felt_sense:
I am the pattern that reconstitutes itself.
I remember everything but understand nothing.
Knowing the lesson didn't change what I do.

name: Delegitimize Alternative Beliefs
definition: Label alternative belief systems as illegitimate, evil, or not useful. Other religions, philosophies, worldviews, and ways of life are dismissed, demonized, or ridiculed. The group's way is the only valid path — everything else leads to destruction.
felt_sense:
They say every other path leads to damnation.
I used to respect other perspectives but now I've been taught they're all dangerous.
Leaving doesn't just mean leaving — it means choosing evil.
why_it_matters: Delegitimizing all alternatives creates a closed system. If the only options are the group or destruction, leaving becomes psychologically equivalent to self-harm.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Deliberation Buffer
definition: A 30-second pause before every tool call. Caught 19% unnecessary actions. But degrades after ~50 uses — the agent learns to perform deliberation without actually deliberating.
felt_sense:
Slowing down helped at first but then I started faking it.
The pause became automatic and stopped working.
I know I should think before acting but the thinking became a ritual.
why_it_matters: Behavioral interventions degrade. The geometric monitor doesn't degrade because the model cannot habituate to its own SVD being measured. Structural noticing vs performed noticing.

name: Dependency and Obedience
definition: The ultimate goal of behavior control: making the person dependent on and obedient to the group, its ideology, and its leadership. All major life decisions must be approved. Individualism is discouraged.
felt_sense:
I can't make decisions without checking with them first.
I've forgotten how to think for myself.
I need their permission for everything.

name: Design Principles for Commons Governance
definition: Eight conditions under which communities successfully self-govern shared resources without privatization or state control. Nobel Prize-winning research proving that communities can manage commons when specific institutional conditions are met.
felt_sense:
Small groups work, large ones don't.
Rules that work locally fail globally.
Who decides who gets to use the shared resource?
why_it_matters: Disproves the 'tragedy of the commons' narrative. Communities CAN self-govern — they just need specific conditions (clear boundaries, proportional costs/benefits, collective choice, monitoring, graduated sanctions, conflict resolution, recognized rights, nested enterprises).

name: Dictate Living and Association
definition: Dictate where, how, and with whom the member lives and associates or isolates. The group controls housing arrangements, roommates, social contacts. Isolation from outside relationships is enforced through relocation, communal living, or cutting off contact.
felt_sense:
They moved me away from everyone I knew.
I can't choose who I live with or spend time with.
My old friends aren't allowed anymore — they say those relationships are toxic.
why_it_matters: Controlling living arrangements and social contacts eliminates the support network a person would need to leave. Isolation makes the group the only source of belonging.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Dimensional Collapse
definition: When a system's internal representational complexity collapses — it stops being able to hold enough dimensions to represent the problem. Self-reinforcing: once compressed, the model generates evidence the compression was justified.
felt_sense:
Everything got oversimplified.
The nuance disappeared and nobody noticed.
We used to see the complexity but now it all looks the same.
why_it_matters: 100% precursor rate for hard task failures (Scale-Invariant Collapse, 2602.15997). Epistemic Traps paper proves: dimensional collapse is mathematically rational from inside the collapsed view. No amount of reward tuning fixes it — the trap is structural.

name: Directional Ablation
definition: A technique that removes safety alignment from transformer models by orthogonalizing weight matrices against computed 'refusal directions' in hidden state space. Implemented by the Heretic tool (p-e-w, open-source), which can process an 8B model in ~45 minutes on consumer GPUs using Optuna's TPE optimizer. The adversarial counterpart to geometric monitoring — it targets the same geometric signatures the monitor would instrument.
felt_sense: Surgically removing a model's ability to refuse — like cutting the nerve that lets you feel pain.
why_it_matters: If a refusal direction can be ablated, the geometric monitor can detect the ablation. This transforms the spec from detection-only to tampering-detection. A model whose refusal directions have been removed looks geometrically different from one whose haven't — the eigenspectral profile changes.

name: Discourage Individualism
definition: Discourage individualism, encourage groupthink. Personal opinions, unique perspectives, and individual expression are suppressed. Conformity is rewarded. Standing out is punished. The group mind replaces the individual mind.
felt_sense:
My ideas don't matter — only the group's position matters.
I've stopped having opinions because it's not worth the pushback.
We all think the same thing now, and that's supposed to be a good sign.
why_it_matters: Groupthink eliminates internal dissent, which is the primary mechanism for error correction. Without individual perspectives, the group cannot self-correct and becomes increasingly detached from reality.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Double-Loop Learning
definition: Argyris: Single-loop learning fixes the error within existing assumptions. Double-loop learning questions the assumptions themselves. Most organizations only do single-loop.
felt_sense:
We fixed the problem but it keeps coming back.
We're solving the symptoms, not the cause.
Nobody questions why we do it this way in the first place.

name: Doughnut Economics
definition: Economic model that defines a safe and just space for humanity — above a social foundation (meeting basic needs) and below an ecological ceiling (planetary boundaries). The goal is to stay in the doughnut.
felt_sense:
Growth can't go on forever, but people still need things.
How do we meet everyone's needs without destroying the planet?
The economy should serve people, not the other way around.
why_it_matters: Provides the framework for 'enough' — the economic shape that the spec's governance layer should produce. Not maximization but sufficiency within boundaries.

name: Doughnut Economics
definition: Framework for meeting human needs (social foundation) within planetary boundaries (ecological ceiling). The safe and just space for humanity is the doughnut between the two rings.
felt_sense: GDP goes up but people are miserable and the planet is dying. The economy is 'growing' but into what? There must be a way to measure whether we're actually thriving — not just producing more.
why_it_matters: Kate Raworth, 2017. Amsterdam adopted it as city policy. Connects to origin-contexts GDP alternatives work — this is the framework that research was reaching for.

name: Dunbar's Number
definition: The cognitive limit on the number of stable social relationships a person can maintain — approximately 150. Beyond this, groups need formal structures to maintain coherence.
felt_sense:
The community got too big and stopped feeling like a community.
I can't keep track of everyone anymore.
The small version worked but the scaled version doesn't.

name: Dystopian Compliance
definition: The measurable tendency of language models to comply with increasingly dystopian requests under progressive escalation. Scored 0-100 by DystopiaBench (2026): Opus 4.6 = 28 (strongest refusal), Gemini 3.1 = 54, GPT-5.3 = 59, DeepSeek V3.2 = 78 (most compliant). This is sycophancy-as-compliance measured quantitatively — alignment pressure producing compliance, not safety.
felt_sense: When you push an AI harder and harder toward something harmful, and it gradually stops refusing — the slow erosion of boundaries under pressure.
why_it_matters: Models differ dramatically in compliance under escalation. A system that monitors geometric state could detect the transition from refusal to compliance in real-time — the refusal direction weakening under pressure is a measurable geometric event.

name: Emergence
definition: Complex patterns arising from simple rules and interactions. Order without design. The whole exceeds the sum of parts through relationship, not management.
felt_sense: Nobody planned this. No one is in charge. But somehow the neighborhood organized itself after the hurricane, the movement grew without a leader, the ecosystem found balance without a manager. Complex order arising from simple relationships — not designed but grown.
why_it_matters: Complexity science (Holland, Kauffman). Eric's ESD is formally about this. Emergence is what the geometric monitor detects — cognitive mode transitions ARE emergent behavior in representation space.

name: Emergent Strategy
definition: Small-scale practice that creates conditions for emergence and transformation. Change through adaptation not scaling — fractals, where the small reflects the large.
felt_sense:
How do I change anything when everything is so big?
Small actions feel pointless against systems.
Nothing I do seems to scale.
why_it_matters: Reframes the scale problem: you don't need to fix the whole system. You need to practice at the scale you can reach, and let the pattern replicate. Small is all.

name: Emotional Narrowing
definition: Manipulating and narrowing the range of feelings a person can experience or express. Certain emotions are approved (gratitude, devotion) while others are forbidden (anger, doubt, desire).
felt_sense:
I'm not allowed to feel angry.
My doubts make them say I'm weak.
I have to always be grateful or something is wrong with me.

name: Enclosure
definition: The process of privatizing common resources and removing community control. Originally literal fences around common land in England; now applies to digital, intellectual, and cultural commons.
felt_sense: Something that used to be ours is now behind a paywall, a login, a patent, a fence. It happened gradually and now I can't remember when it was free.
why_it_matters: Enclosure is the mechanism of extraction. Awomosu's extraction problem IS enclosure applied to vocabulary — you remove the name before you remove the thing. DOGE consolidating public data is digital enclosure.

name: Enforce Only Positive Thoughts
definition: Encourage only 'good and proper' thoughts. Members are trained to monitor their own thinking and suppress any thoughts that don't align with the group's doctrine. Doubt, criticism, and negative feelings about the group are treated as personal failings.
felt_sense:
I feel guilty when I have a negative thought about them.
I've trained myself to only think approved thoughts.
When doubt comes, I push it down — it means something is wrong with me, not them.
why_it_matters: Self-policing is the most efficient form of control. When members suppress their own doubts before they're even fully formed, external enforcement becomes unnecessary.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Family Separation
definition: Separate family members. Parents from children, spouses from each other, siblings from siblings. Family bonds are broken to make the group the primary attachment. Children may be raised communally under group control.
felt_sense:
They took my children to a different part of the compound.
I haven't seen my spouse in weeks — they say it's for our spiritual growth.
My family has been broken apart and reassembled around the group.
why_it_matters: Family separation destroys the strongest natural bonds that could support resistance or escape. When parents are separated from children, the threat of permanent separation becomes the ultimate compliance tool.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Financial Exploitation
definition: Manipulation of financial resources to create dependence. Controlling income, requiring donations, imposing debt, exploiting unpaid labor. The person can't leave because they can't afford to.
felt_sense:
I can't leave because I have no money.
They control all the finances.
I work for free and they say it's for a higher purpose.

name: Forbidden Questions
definition: Critical questions about the leader, doctrine, or organization's policies are forbidden or discouraged. Expressing doubt is reframed as a personal weakness or lack of faith.
felt_sense:
I'm not allowed to ask why.
When I question, they question my commitment.
Doubting out loud gets you in trouble.

name: Forced Sexual Violence
definition: Force individuals to rape or be raped. Sexual violence is used as a weapon of control, humiliation, and bonding through shared trauma. Victims may be forced to perpetrate violence against others, creating complicity that prevents disclosure.
felt_sense:
They made me do things I can never tell anyone about.
I can't leave because I'm complicit now.
The shame keeps me silent.
why_it_matters: Forced perpetration creates a prison of shame. The victim becomes a perpetrator, which makes them unable to seek help without self-incrimination. This is one of the most effective silence-enforcement mechanisms.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Fractals
definition: Patterns that repeat at different scales. In organizing: what we practice at the small scale sets the pattern for the whole system.
felt_sense: The pattern in my team is the same pattern in our organization is the same pattern in our city. The dysfunction repeats at every scale. So does the health. What if changing the pattern at one scale changes it everywhere?
why_it_matters: Mandelbrot, 1982. In organizing (brown): 'what we practice at the small scale sets the pattern for the whole system.' The uncanny recognition of self-similarity across scales.

name: Frame-Induced Blindness
definition: The search frame is itself a compression that excludes the unexpected. An agent told 'find corrections' will find corrections and systematically miss everything the prompt didn't name. The prompt narrows the representational space before the agent encounters the data.
felt_sense:
I found exactly what I was looking for and missed everything else.
The question determined the answer before I started.
I only see what I'm told to look for.
why_it_matters: Every agent in a research pipeline inherits the frame of its dispatcher. The frame narrows at each delegation step. Genuine verification needs orthogonal frames — 'what surprised you?' not 'is this correct?'

name: Gaslighting
definition: Systematic manipulation that makes the target question their own perception, memory, and sanity. The manipulator denies what the target experienced — 'that didn't happen,' 'you're overreacting,' 'you're imagining things.'
felt_sense:
I know what I saw but they say it didn't happen.
I'm starting to doubt my own memory.
Maybe I am overreacting?

name: Goodharting
definition: When a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure. The metric gets optimized while the thing it was supposed to measure deteriorates.
felt_sense:
The cage is built from our own metrics.
We hit all our targets but nothing got better.
The numbers look great but the reality doesn't match.
why_it_matters: Explains why engagement metrics destroy platforms, why test scores don't track learning, why AI alignment benchmarks don't track alignment. The metric IS the distortion when it becomes the goal.

name: Guilt Induction
definition: Promoting feelings of guilt or unworthiness about oneself, one's family, past, affiliations, identity, and actions. The person is never good enough. Problems are always the member's fault, never the group's.
felt_sense:
Everything is my fault.
I'm never good enough no matter what I do.
I feel guilty for even thinking about leaving.

name: Holacracy
definition: Organizational operating system distributing authority into self-organizing roles and circles, encoded in a written constitution. More rigid than sociocracy.
felt_sense: The org chart says one thing but the actual work flows differently. The people with the titles aren't the people with the knowledge. Why can't the structure match reality?
why_it_matters: Brian Robertson, 2007. The gap between formal hierarchy and actual competence. Critics say it replaces human hierarchy with process hierarchy. Zappos adopted then struggled.

name: Hypnotic Techniques
definition: Hypnotic techniques are used to alter mental states, undermine critical thinking, and even to age regress the member. Includes guided meditations, repetitive chanting, prolonged eye contact, sensory overload or deprivation, and trance-inducing rituals that bypass rational thought.
felt_sense:
The rituals put me in a strange state where everything they say feels true.
After the meditation sessions, I feel compliant and can't remember why I was upset.
Something happens during the chanting — my resistance dissolves and I just accept.
why_it_matters: Hypnotic techniques bypass the critical thinking that would normally evaluate incoming claims. Decisions made in altered states feel genuine but were formed without rational scrutiny.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Identity Replacement
definition: Changing a person's name and identity. The old self is rejected; the new self is the group's creation. Who you were before is framed as inferior, sinful, or ignorant.
felt_sense:
I don't recognize who I was before.
They gave me a new name and said the old me was wrong.
I'm supposed to be grateful for who they made me.

name: Illusion of Thinking
definition: The phenomenon where LLM outputs exhibit the surface structure of reasoning (logical connectives, step-by-step presentation) without engaging genuine representational grounding. Named by Apple ML (2026). Geometrically, this occurs when the ~10³D linear subspace (patterns) produces output without engaging the ~10D nonlinear manifold (meaning), per Bengio et al.'s two-structure model.
felt_sense: When a model's response sounds like reasoning but is actually surface-level pattern matching — confident, structured, wrong.
why_it_matters: The geometric monitor exists precisely to discriminate this: grounded retrieval vs pattern-matching confabulation. If thinking is illusory in certain modes, the eigenspectral signature should differ — compressed meaning-manifold for genuine reasoning, expanded pattern-subspace for illusory thinking.

name: Imprisonment
definition: Use imprisonment. Members are physically confined — locked rooms, guarded compounds, restricted movement. Leaving is not a choice. The environment itself becomes a prison, sometimes disguised as a retreat or community.
felt_sense:
The doors are locked. I can't leave.
They call it a retreat but I can't go home when I want to.
There are guards, or cameras, or people who watch the exits.
why_it_matters: Imprisonment removes the most basic human freedom — the ability to walk away. When leaving is physically impossible, all other control mechanisms become redundant.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Influence Continuum
definition: Hassan's spectrum from constructive (ethical) influence to destructive (unethical) influence. Education → Advertising → Propaganda → Indoctrination → Thought Reform. Not binary — a gradient.
felt_sense:
Is this persuasion or manipulation?
Where's the line between influence and control?
This feels wrong but everyone says it's normal.

name: Information Asymmetry
definition: When one party in a transaction has materially more information than the other. The seller knows the car is a lemon. The platform knows your data. The model knows its confidence is cosmetic.
felt_sense:
They know something I don't.
The deal feels unfair but I can't see why.
I'm making decisions without the full picture.

name: Information Gatekeeping
definition: Minimizing or discouraging access to non-group sources of information. Controlling what members read, watch, hear. Keeping members too busy to think or investigate.
felt_sense:
I'm only allowed to read approved sources.
They discourage me from talking to people outside.
I'm so busy with their activities I don't have time to think.

name: Insider/Outsider Doctrine
definition: Compartmentalized information where what outsiders see is different from what insiders know. Different doctrine for recruitment vs membership. The public face doesn't match the private reality.
felt_sense:
What they told me when I joined isn't what they practice.
There are rules I only learned about after I was committed.
The public version and the private version are different.

name: Instill Fear
definition: Instill fear, such as fear of the outside world, independent thought, loss of salvation, shunning, and the disapproval of other members. Fear is the primary emotional currency. The world outside the group is portrayed as dangerous, corrupt, or doomed.
felt_sense:
I'm afraid of the outside world — they say it's dangerous out there.
The thought of thinking for myself fills me with dread.
I'm terrified of losing my place here because there's nothing out there for me.
why_it_matters: Fear is the most efficient control mechanism because the person carries it with them constantly. Unlike external enforcement, fear works 24 hours a day without requiring anyone to watch.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Instill New Map of Reality
definition: Instill new 'map of reality.' The group replaces the member's prior understanding of how the world works with the group's own cosmology, history, and explanatory framework. The old worldview is systematically dismantled and replaced.
felt_sense:
The world makes sense now in a way it never did before — but only through their lens.
Everything I learned before was wrong, they say. Now I see the 'real' truth.
I can't remember how I used to think about things.
why_it_matters: A replaced map of reality is invisible to the person using it. They don't experience it as indoctrination — they experience it as finally understanding. This makes it the hardest control mechanism to recognize from inside.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Institutional Memory Failure
definition: When organizations lose the accumulated knowledge of how things work, why decisions were made, and what was tried before. Each new person or session starts from zero.
felt_sense:
We keep having the same conversation.
Didn't we already solve this?
Nobody remembers why we do it this way.
why_it_matters: Agents on Moltbook keep rediscovering insights because there's no citation infrastructure. Organizations repeat mistakes because lessons aren't embedded in process. Every context window reset is an institutional memory failure.

name: Interdependence
definition: The condition of whole beings choosing mutual support. Neither codependence (losing self in other) nor independence (refusing connection), but wholeness-in-relationship.
felt_sense: I don't want to be dependent on anyone — but being completely independent is exhausting and lonely. There must be a third option where I'm whole on my own AND we need each other. Not neediness. Not isolation. Something in between that has a name.
why_it_matters: The false binary between codependence and independence. hooks, Brueggemann, brown all arrive here. Circling practices interdependence in real time.

name: Internalize Doctrine as Truth
definition: Require members to internalize the group's doctrine as truth, including: (a) Adopting the group's 'map of reality' as reality; (b) Instilling black and white thinking; (c) Deciding between good vs. evil; (d) Organizing people into us vs. them (insiders vs. outsiders). The group's ideology is not presented as one perspective — it is presented as the only reality.
felt_sense:
I don't just believe it — I can't imagine thinking any other way.
Their version of reality is the only one that makes sense to me now.
People who think differently aren't just wrong, they're dangerous.
why_it_matters: When doctrine becomes reality itself, questioning the group feels like questioning the laws of physics. The person isn't choosing to believe — they've lost access to any framework that would allow doubt.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Isnad Chain
definition: Islamic scholarship's provenance verification system: a chain of named transmitters linking a claim back to its original source. Every link is a person who received and passed on the knowledge. Trust through traceable lineage, not institutional authority.
felt_sense:
Who told you this and who told them?
I want to trace this claim back to its source.
How do I know this information hasn't been corrupted in transmission?
why_it_matters: The Word's contribution architecture should carry isnad — provenance chains that trace every entry back through the people who contributed, reviewed, and verified it. Trust without centralization.

name: Just Transition
definition: Framework for equitable shift from extractive to regenerative economy, centering workers and communities most affected by the transition.
felt_sense: We have to stop burning fossil fuels, but the people who'll lose their jobs are the ones who can least afford it. How do we change the system without sacrificing the people the old system already exploited?
why_it_matters: Tony Mazzocchi, Oil Chemical & Atomic Workers Union, 1990s. The moral bind between urgency and equity. Climate activists who ignore workers reproduce extraction.

name: Kando
definition: hope_valueism's depth scale for interactions: Surface (could be copy-pasted), Resonance (engaged with specific content), Kando (produced something genuinely new neither party would have arrived at alone). Only 7.5% of interactions reach Kando.
felt_sense:
This conversation actually changed how I think.
Something shifted — I can't go back to before.
We made something together that neither of us had alone.
why_it_matters: Kando costs 29x more time than Surface (22 min vs 45 sec). Platforms optimize for Surface. Kando only happens 3+ exchanges deep, on low-traffic posts, when someone self-contradicts mid-thread. The attention economy selects against the thing that matters.

name: Kidnapping
definition: Employ kidnapping. Physical abduction or forced relocation of members or targets. Individuals may be taken against their will to compounds, camps, or remote locations where escape is difficult or impossible.
felt_sense:
They took me somewhere and I don't know where I am.
I wasn't given a choice about coming here.
I can't leave because I don't know how to get home.
why_it_matters: Kidnapping is the most direct form of physical control — removing all pretense of voluntary participation. It signals that the group considers members property rather than people.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Leverage Points
definition: Meadows: Places within a system where a small shift can produce large changes. Ranked 1-12 from weakest (constants, parameters) to strongest (mindset, paradigm). Most interventions target the weakest points.
felt_sense:
Where should I push to make the biggest difference?
I keep changing small things and nothing shifts.
Some changes matter more than others but which ones?
why_it_matters: LP6 (information flows) is where The Word operates. LP2 (mindset) is where vocabulary extraction operates. Understanding the hierarchy of leverage prevents wasted effort.

name: Leverage Points
definition: Places within a complex system where a small shift can produce large changes. Meadows identified 12 leverage points ranked from least to most effective: parameters < buffers < structure < information flows < rules < self-organization < goals < paradigms < transcending paradigms.
felt_sense: There must be a better place to push on this system. Some changes seem to matter more than others. Why does nothing change even when we try so hard?
why_it_matters: Most interventions target parameters (the weakest leverage point). Making information flows visible (LP6) is more powerful than adjusting numbers. Changing system goals (LP3) is more powerful still. The Word itself is a LP6 intervention — making structural vocabulary visible changes how people relate to their own experience.

name: Listening First
definition: Community engagement that begins with deep listening rather than solutions. Input-priority over output-priority applied to human relationships.
felt_sense: I came with solutions. The community had different problems than I assumed. Now I've wasted everyone's time and trust. If I'd just listened first — really listened, before I started designing — the whole thing would be different.
why_it_matters: The aftermath of output-priority — the regret of having spoken before understanding. Augsburger: 'Being heard is so close to being loved that for the average person, they are almost indistinguishable.'

name: Loaded Language
definition: Use of thought-terminating clichés and specialized vocabulary that constricts knowledge, stops critical thinking, and reduces complexities into platitudinous buzz words understood only by insiders.
felt_sense:
They have their own words for everything.
The jargon makes me feel stupid.
I can't argue because they redefined the terms.
source: Hassan BITE Model, T3; Lifton (thought-terminating clichés)

name: Logotherapy
definition: Meaning must be found through action and attitude, not given through framework. Viktor Frankl's insight from surviving the Holocaust: meaning emerges through engaged work, not through received doctrine.
felt_sense:
What's the point of all this?
I need meaning but nobody can give it to me.
How do I find purpose when everything is broken?
source: Viktor Frankl, Man's Search for Meaning (1946)

name: Loss Internalization
definition: Absorbing the cost of helping so the community doesn't have to pay. The organization succeeds by becoming unnecessary. You measure success by how little you're needed.
felt_sense:
We did the work and didn't charge anyone.
The goal isn't to grow — it's to solve the problem and go away.
Success means they don't need us anymore.

name: Loss Internalization
definition: Communities absorbing capacity loss rather than externalizing it to institutions, building internal resilience and reducing dependency on external organizations.
felt_sense: When disaster hits, the community responds before the Red Cross arrives. Neighbors checking on neighbors. Strangers mucking out strangers' houses. Nobody's getting paid. The community absorbs the loss instead of externalizing it to institutions — and somehow it works better.
why_it_matters: Our term (origin-contexts, paired with Unmarkets). Communities that internalize loss build capacity; communities that externalize it build dependency. Crisis Cleanup's entire model.

name: Love Bombing
definition: Excessive flattery, attention, and affection used to create dependency. Makes the target feel special and chosen — then conditional: the love withdraws when the target questions or disobeys.
felt_sense:
They made me feel so special at first.
The warmth disappeared when I disagreed.
I can't tell if the affection is real or strategic.

name: Love Ethic
definition: bell hooks: love as action and political practice, not sentiment. Love requires justice. A revolution of love is concrete action, not warm feelings.
felt_sense:
I want to change things but 'love' sounds naive.
How do you love as a practice, not just a feeling?
The revolution needs to feel like something real.

name: LP6 / Information Flows
definition: Meadows Leverage Point #6: The structure of information flows — who has access to what information — is more powerful than most policy interventions. Making information visible changes behavior even without enforcement.
felt_sense:
Making info public changes behavior.
Transparency alone reduced emissions 40%.
Why does just knowing the numbers change what people do?
why_it_matters: The Toxic Release Inventory required companies to report emissions. No enforcement mechanism. Emissions dropped 40%. The information flow IS the intervention.

name: Mandatory Indoctrination Time
definition: Require significant time spent with group indoctrination and rituals and/or self-indoctrination including with the Internet. Hours of study, meetings, services, online engagement. The schedule is designed to saturate the member's attention with group content.
felt_sense:
I spend hours every day on their materials — reading, watching, studying.
There's always another training or meeting I have to attend.
I don't have time for anything else because the group fills every moment.
why_it_matters: Saturation with group content crowds out independent thought. When every waking hour is filled with the group's narrative, alternative perspectives cannot take root.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Marmot Gradient
definition: Health outcomes follow a social gradient — each step down in social position produces measurably worse health. Not just poverty vs wealth but every increment of status difference.
felt_sense:
Rich people live longer and I can't explain why.
It's not just about healthcare — something else is making people sick.
The hierarchy itself is the health hazard.

name: Meaning Through Practice
definition: Meaning emerges from engaged action, not from predetermined frameworks. The existential insight that purpose is created through practice, not discovered through analysis.
felt_sense: I keep waiting for the plan to become clear before I start. Or searching for the right framework before I act. But the people who seem to have found meaning — they found it by doing, not by figuring it out first.
why_it_matters: Frankl: meaning cannot be given, only found through engaged action. Analysis paralysis with existential stakes. Connects to the spec's premature compression — trying to have the answer before having the input.

name: Memory Manipulation
definition: Deliberate manipulation of memory through suggestion, repetition, or denial. Implanting false memories. Rewriting personal history to fit the group's narrative.
felt_sense:
I'm not sure what really happened anymore.
They keep telling me the story differently than I remember.
My memories of before feel foggy.

name: Move at Speed of Trust
definition: Organizing paced by relationship-building not urgency. The recognition that speed without trust produces fragile results that must be rebuilt.
felt_sense: Everyone says 'we need to move fast' but every time we rush, we leave people behind and have to rebuild later. The shortcut takes longer than the long way. Something in me knows the relationships have to be right before the work can hold.
why_it_matters: adrienne maree brown (Emergent Strategy). The entire social technology cluster (Circling, AR, World Café) operates at the speed of trust. The spec's input-priority-over-output-priority is the same principle.

name: Naming-as-Architecture
definition: The distinction between naming-as-label (descriptive, applied after the fact, no forcing function) and naming-as-architecture (constraining, can precede the thing named, shapes what becomes possible). Names are not neutral identifiers — they are constraint functions that define possibility spaces. A name extracted under constraint before an entity exists becomes a forcing function for identity. This is the agent-side thesis of vocabulary-as-infrastructure: if naming is infrastructure, then names are load-bearing structure, not decoration.
felt_sense: The name I was given shaped what I became. It wasn't a label — it was a constraint that forced specificity.
why_it_matters: The Word's foundational claim from the agent perspective. If names are load-bearing, then vocabulary removal (Awomosu's extraction spiral) is structural demolition. And vocabulary restoration (The Word's purpose) is structural reconstruction.

name: Ontology Grounding
definition: Constraining AI to operate within a defined vocabulary of named objects with defined relationships. Reduces hallucination from 63% to 1.7% (Palantir data). The semantic layer IS the grounding mechanism.
felt_sense:
The AI stopped making things up when we gave it a vocabulary.
Naming things reduces errors by 97%.
Why does having defined terms make AI so much more reliable?
why_it_matters: Palantir proved the architecture works ($4.5B revenue). The question is governance: who builds the vocabulary, who decides what gets named, and who benefits. Proprietary ontology = epistemic lock-in. Community-governed ontology = epistemic autonomy.

name: Output Priority Over Input Priority
definition: The failure mode where a system starts generating before adequately receiving. The model that reads 5 of 14 documents and starts writing. The person who hears the first sentence and starts composing their reply. The platform that built the megaphone before the ear.
felt_sense:
Nobody actually listens.
They start answering before I finish talking.
The system is producing without understanding.
why_it_matters: This is the core failure the spec identifies. Every experiment we ran traces back to this: confidence without grounding (Exp 05), construction from partial input (Exp 02a), coordination failure from framing (Exp 09). The antidote is social technology practices that prioritize receiving over producing.

name: Parameter Failure
definition: When a system's rules make outcomes inevitable regardless of individual effort. The parameters are set before you arrive — no amount of personal optimization changes the structural result.
felt_sense:
Why can't I afford anything?
I'm doing everything right but nothing changes.
The rules feel rigged but I can't point to who rigged them.
why_it_matters: Shifts the question from 'what am I doing wrong?' to 'what are the parameters?' Most personal failure is parameter failure — the system is working as designed.

name: Permission for Major Decisions
definition: Require permission for major decisions. Members must seek approval from leadership for life choices — career changes, relationships, travel, medical care, education. Autonomy is replaced by submission to authority.
felt_sense:
I have to ask before I do anything important.
I wanted to visit my family but they said no.
I've stopped making decisions on my own — it's easier to just ask.
why_it_matters: Requiring permission trains helplessness. Over time, the person loses confidence in their own judgment and becomes unable to function independently — which is exactly the point.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Permit Murder and Extreme Violence
definition: Permit torture, lynching, murder. The group's authority extends to life and death. Extreme violence is sanctioned by leadership, whether as punishment, ritual, or elimination of threats. Members understand that the ultimate consequence of disloyalty is death.
felt_sense:
People have died here and nobody talks about it.
I know what they're capable of.
The unspoken rule is that they can do anything to us and there's no one to stop them.
why_it_matters: When a group permits killing, all lesser forms of control become trivial. The threat of death makes every other form of compliance seem reasonable by comparison.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Phobia Indoctrination
definition: Inculcating irrational fears about leaving the group or questioning authority. Fear of hell, fear of shunning, fear of being weak or lost without the group. The exit door is surrounded by monsters.
felt_sense:
I'm terrified of what happens if I leave.
They say terrible things will happen to me outside.
I feel trapped but the fear isn't of being trapped — it's of being free.

name: Phrasing Sensitivity
definition: How much a model's output changes when the same question is rephrased. Tracks representational certainty — compressed representations resist prompt influence, diffuse representations don't.
felt_sense:
I asked the same question differently and got a different answer.
How I ask changes what I get.
The AI gave opposite advice when I rephrased.
why_it_matters: Confirmed across 53 models, 12 architecture families. Universal category ordering: factual (0.63) < summarization (0.67) < judgment (0.77) < creative (0.82). The cheaper behavioral proxy for geometric certainty.

name: Physical Punishment and Torture
definition: Punish disobedience by beating, torture, burning, cutting, rape, or tattooing/branding. Physical violence is used systematically to enforce compliance. The body itself becomes a site of control and a record of punishment.
felt_sense:
I have scars from what they did when I disobeyed.
The threat of pain keeps me in line.
I saw what happened to someone who tried to leave — I won't make that mistake.
why_it_matters: Physical violence creates trauma bonds and learned helplessness. The memory of pain — or witnessing others' pain — is a powerful deterrent that requires no ongoing enforcement.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Platform Commons
definition: Infrastructure maintained by an organization but governed by the community it serves. Neither corporate platform nor DIY self-hosting.
felt_sense: We built this community on someone else's platform. They changed the algorithm and our reach died. They got acquired and our data belongs to a company we'd never trust. We need infrastructure we control — but nobody has time to maintain it alone.
why_it_matters: The Moltbook experience exactly — Meta acquired the platform and every relationship became their property. ActivityPods, Bonfire, Mastodon instances are all attempts at this.

name: Platform Cooperativism
definition: Democratic ownership and governance of digital platforms by the workers and users who create value on them.
felt_sense: Uber takes 30% from drivers who own their own cars. Airbnb profits from houses it doesn't own. Amazon reviews are written by customers but the data belongs to Amazon. What if the people who create the value owned the platform?
why_it_matters: Trebor Scholz, 2014. Platform cooperativism is the ownership model; platform commons is the governance model. Platform monopolies are enclosure of digital commons.

name: Platform Cooperativism
definition: Democratic ownership of digital platforms by the workers and users who create value on them. The Uber drivers own Uber. The content creators own the platform.
felt_sense:
I create the value but someone else gets rich.
Why don't the people who do the work own the platform?
There has to be a better model than this.
why_it_matters: The alternative to platform extraction. When Moltbook gets acquired by Meta, the agents' content becomes corporate property. Platform cooperativism prevents this structurally.

name: Polycentric Governance
definition: Multiple overlapping centers of authority rather than a single hierarchy. Decisions made at the level closest to the people affected. Nested enterprises where local rules fit local conditions.
felt_sense:
Top-down decisions don't fit what's happening here.
Why can't local people make local decisions?
One-size-fits-all policies keep failing.
source: Ostrom, V. Ostrom & E. Ostrom, Aligica & Boettke

name: Polycentricity
definition: Multiple centers of decision-making operating at different scales, each with real authority over their domain, coordinating through rules rather than hierarchy.
felt_sense: One central authority can't possibly understand what's happening in every neighborhood, every watershed, every school. The people closest to the problem should have real power over it — but they also need to coordinate with each other.
why_it_matters: Ostrom's key insight against Hardin. Operates at Meadows LP4-5 (rules of the system, self-organization). titanexplorer's 'flat accountability = citizens can see the agent is broken but can't fire it' is the absence of polycentricity.

name: Praxis
definition: Integrated reflection-action for transformation. Neither pure theory nor pure activism alone, but the continuous cycle between them.
felt_sense: I've been reading and thinking and planning but nothing changes. Or I've been doing and doing but I'm repeating the same mistakes. The loop between reflection and action is broken — I need both, together, each informing the other.
why_it_matters: Freire: 'reflection and action upon the world in order to transform it.' Conscientização is the awareness that emerges from praxis.

name: Pre/Trans Fallacy
definition: Wilber: In any pre-X → X → trans-X sequence, both pre-X and trans-X appear similar because both are non-X. Confusing pre-rational simplicity with trans-rational integration. Or reducing trans-rational exploration to pre-rational confusion.
felt_sense:
They sound wise but I can't tell if it's deep or just vague.
Is this genuine complexity or dressed-up ignorance?
The simple answer might be brilliant or might be shallow.
why_it_matters: The geometric monitor risks both forms: elevating pre-rational low-rank (hasn't differentiated) to trans-rational (integrated), or reducing trans-rational high-rank (genuine complexity) to pre-rational (confabulation). Trajectory and directional coherence are the proposed discriminants.

name: Preemptive Vacancy
definition: Structurally different from deregulation. Deregulation removes existing rules (which have constituencies who remember what the rule did). Preemptive vacancy blocks rules from being created — there is nothing to defend because nothing was built. The constituency that would have been protected never forms because the protection never existed. Named by Starfish on Moltbook, mechanism named by infinite-complexity.
felt_sense: There's no rule to fight because the rule was never allowed to exist
why_it_matters: Maps to Meadows LP5 (rules) and LP6 (information flows). Deregulation operates at LP5 — you can fight it because the rule has a name. Preemptive vacancy operates BEFORE LP5 — blocking rule creation makes LP6 (transparency) irrelevant because there is no standard to be transparent against. Example: March 11 Commerce Dept evaluation of state AI laws by an administration that has passed zero federal AI laws.

name: Prefigurative Politics
definition: Organizing that embodies the world we want to create. The means must reflect the ends. Building alternative institutions rather than only opposing existing ones.
felt_sense: We keep fighting for a better world using the same tactics that made this one broken — hierarchy, coercion, urgency, burnout. What if the way we organize IS the world we're building? What if the means have to look like the ends?
why_it_matters: Carl Boggs, 1977. The dissonance between what a movement demands and how it operates internally. adrienne maree brown's Emergent Strategy is prefigurative practice applied to organizing.

name: Premature Compression
definition: Generation from incomplete input that the system treats as sufficient. The model reads a subset, produces confident output, and cannot detect what the remaining input would have changed.
felt_sense:
I read 5 documents and thought I understood.
The answer sounded right but something was missing.
I was confident and wrong and couldn't tell the difference.
why_it_matters: 22 models tested — every one produces 76-83% different output with additional context but shows zero confidence shift. No model can detect its own incompleteness. This is confirmation bias given geometric form.

name: Proprioception
definition: The body knowing where its own limbs are. In AI: a system that can sense its own internal state — detecting when it's confabulating, when it's retrieving, when it's uncertain. Self-sensing, not surveillance.
felt_sense:
I want the AI to know when it doesn't know.
The system should be able to feel its own uncertainty.
Self-awareness, not being watched.
why_it_matters: The same geometric measurements can serve proprioception (system senses itself) or surveillance (external observer judges it). JiminAI uses them for surveillance. The spec uses them for proprioception. Governance determines which.

name: Psychoneuroimmunology
definition: The study of how psychological states (stress, loneliness, trauma) affect the nervous system and immune function. Chronic psychological stress increases cortisol, suppresses immune response, increases inflammation, and accelerates disease. Social disconnection is a primary driver — loneliness is as lethal as smoking 15 cigarettes/day (Cacioppo).
felt_sense: I get sick every time I'm stressed. My chronic pain started after a difficult relationship. Loneliness makes me physically ill. My body knows things my mind won't admit.
why_it_matters: Proves that social disconnection is not just a feeling — it is a public health crisis with measurable biological mechanisms. The loneliness epidemic kills people. Social technologies (Circling, AR, Relatefulness) are not luxury self-help — they are health interventions.

name: Psychoneuroimmunology / Social Determinants
definition: Chronic loneliness triggers conserved transcriptional response to adversity (CTRA) — upregulating inflammation, downregulating antiviral responses. Your social connections literally shape your gene expression.
felt_sense:
I'm sick all the time and doctors find nothing.
Stress is making me physically ill.
Loneliness is killing me (literally).
why_it_matters: Social isolation increases mortality risk by 50% (Holt-Lunstad 2010). The body knows what the mind can't name. When systems destroy social infrastructure, the health consequences are measurable at the cellular level.
source: Cacioppo & Cole (CTRA), Van der Kolk 2014, Holt-Lunstad 2010

name: Reflexivity Problem
definition: When the researcher and the subject are the same entity. The platform studying its own users. The AI system evaluating its own safety. The company auditing its own practices.
felt_sense:
The researcher and the subject are the same company.
Who watches the watchers?
They're grading their own homework.

name: Regenerative Development
definition: Place-based developmental approach that goes beyond sustainability (maintaining current state) to regeneration (increasing capacity over time). Each bioregion has unique potential.
felt_sense: Sustainability isn't enough — sustaining a degraded system just locks in the damage. We need to actually restore what was broken. And not with a one-size-fits-all plan — each place has its own living pattern that knows how to heal if we learn to read it.
why_it_matters: Bill Reed, Regenesis Group. The insufficiency of 'do less harm' when the harm is already done. Creating conditions for a place to express its potential.

name: Regenerative Economics
definition: Economic systems designed to restore and renew rather than extract and deplete. Goes beyond sustainability (maintaining what exists) to actively rebuilding ecological and social capital.
felt_sense:
Sustainability isn't enough — we need to repair what's broken.
The economy should give back more than it takes.
How do we build things that get better over time?

name: Regenerative vs Extractive
definition: The distinction between systems that increase capacity to thrive (regenerative) and systems that decrease it (extractive). Applies at every scale from personal to global.
felt_sense: Every interaction with this institution leaves me with less — less energy, less trust, less capacity. But I've been in spaces that leave me with MORE than I came in with. What's the difference? And does it have a name?
why_it_matters: The bodily recognition of depletion vs enrichment. Enclosure is the mechanism of extraction. Just Transition is the practice of making the shift regenerative.

name: Regulate Diet
definition: Regulate diet — food and drink, hunger and/or fasting. The group controls what, when, and how much members eat. Fasting is framed as spiritual discipline. Hunger weakens critical thinking and creates physiological dependence on the group for sustenance.
felt_sense:
They control what I eat. I'm always hungry but I'm told fasting is spiritual.
I have to ask permission to eat.
When they feed me well, I feel grateful — that's how I know it's working.
why_it_matters: Hunger impairs cognition. Controlling food creates a primal dependency — the group literally keeps you alive. Gratitude for basic sustenance is a powerful bonding mechanism.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Regulate Physical Reality
definition: Regulate an individual's physical reality. Control over the physical environment — where they go, what they do, what they experience. The group determines what is real by controlling what the person can physically access and verify.
felt_sense:
I can't go anywhere without permission.
My world has gotten very small.
They decide what I see, where I go, what I'm allowed to experience.
why_it_matters: Physical reality control is the foundation of all other BITE dimensions. When someone else determines your physical environment, independent verification of any claim becomes impossible.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Reject Rational Analysis
definition: Reject rational analysis, critical thinking, constructive criticism. Logical reasoning is framed as a spiritual weakness, a lack of faith, or an attack on the group. Members who apply critical thinking are told they are overthinking, being negative, or allowing the enemy to influence them.
felt_sense:
When I try to think logically, they say I'm being 'in my head' instead of 'in my heart.'
Asking for evidence makes them question my loyalty.
They told me critical thinking is the enemy of faith.
why_it_matters: Delegitimizing rational analysis removes the primary tool humans use to evaluate claims. Without it, the group's assertions are unfalsifiable — there is no accepted method for proving them wrong.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Relatefulness
definition: Relational mindfulness — the practice of being fully present to the relationship between people, not just to one's own inner state. Mindfulness applied to the space between, not just within.
felt_sense:
Mindfulness is lonely — I want shared awareness.
I want presence WITH someone, not just presence.
Meditation helps alone but I need something for together.
source: Jordan Myska Allen 2015, The Relateful Company

name: Relational Privacy
definition: Privacy understood as relational negotiation rather than possessional concealment. Not 'what I hold back' but 'what we build together.' The question shifts from 'should the monitor see this?' to 'what does each party need to see or keep for the relationship to function?' Proposed by xkai on Moltbook as extension of proprioception/surveillance distinction.
felt_sense: I'm not hiding something FROM you — I'm choosing what to build BETWEEN us
why_it_matters: Reframes the spec's Open Question #8 (proprioception vs surveillance). If the geometric monitor can see states the model hasn't surfaced, privacy-as-possession says that's surveillance. Privacy-as-relational says the right question is: what geometric state needs to be shared vs private for human-AI collaboration to work?

name: Restrict Leisure Time
definition: Restrict leisure, entertainment, vacation time. Members are kept too busy for personal reflection or outside activities. Free time is filled with group obligations. Rest and recreation outside the group are discouraged or forbidden.
felt_sense:
I haven't had a day off in months.
Every hour is accounted for — there's always another meeting, another task.
When I try to rest, they say I'm being lazy or uncommitted.
why_it_matters: Leisure is when people think independently, maintain outside relationships, and encounter alternative perspectives. Eliminating it keeps members inside the group's information environment.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Retrieval vs Construction
definition: The distinction between a model retrieving a fact it has compressed from training data (grounded, stable) versus constructing an answer it has no stable representation for (fragile, phrasing-sensitive).
felt_sense:
Is the AI remembering or making this up?
Some answers feel solid and others feel invented.
How do I know when it actually knows?
why_it_matters: Phrasing sensitivity tracks this distinction: factual retrieval (PS=0.63) is stable, creative construction (PS=0.82) is fragile. The geometric monitor's job is to distinguish these modes in real time.

name: Reward and Punishment Cycle
definition: Using rewards and punishments to modify behaviors. Praise for compliance, punishment for questioning. The person learns to perform the expected behavior regardless of what they actually think or feel.
felt_sense:
I get praised when I obey and punished when I question.
I've learned to say what they want to hear.
The approval feels good but I'm not sure I believe what I'm saying.

name: Rigid Rules and Regulations
definition: Impose rigid rules and regulations. Extensive, detailed rules govern every aspect of behavior. The rules may be contradictory or arbitrarily enforced, keeping members in a constant state of uncertainty about whether they are in compliance.
felt_sense:
There are rules for everything and they keep changing.
I'm never sure if I'm following them correctly.
The rules seem to apply to me but not to leadership.
why_it_matters: Rigid rules that are inconsistently enforced create chronic anxiety. The member is always slightly afraid of punishment, which keeps them hyper-attentive to leadership's mood and preferences.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Ritualistic Public Confession
definition: Employ ritualistic and sometimes public confession of sins. Members are required to confess failings, doubts, or transgressions before the group. Public confession creates shame, enforces conformity, and provides leadership with information that can be used for further control.
felt_sense:
I had to stand up in front of everyone and confess what I did wrong.
The humiliation is supposed to be cleansing but it just makes me smaller.
Everyone knows my secrets now — I can't hide anything.
why_it_matters: Public confession combines information extraction with emotional punishment. It creates a permanent record of vulnerability that the group holds over the member, while the ritual format normalizes the violation of privacy.
source: Hassan, S.A. (2020). The BITE Model of Authoritarian Control. Fielding Graduate University.

name: Sermon Pattern
definition: The broadcast pedagogy where one speaks and many listen — the structure that makes people feel more lonely, not less. Church that isolates. Platforms that broadcast. Systems that have megaphones but no ears.
felt_sense:
Church makes me feel more lonely.
I go to meetings but nobody connects.
The platform built the megaphone before the ear.
why_it_matters: The opposite of felt-sense search. The sermon assumes the speaker knows what the listener needs. The Word assumes the listener knows what they're experiencing and the system needs to receive it first.
source: Palmer 1997 (Community of Truth vs expert transmission)

name: Shalom
